data.acceptance:
1. createTakeRuntimeBundle in terminal-manager-take-child.ts wires an onTurnEnded handler into createSessionRuntime.
2. Handler logic: when a turn ends in the take-loop, check current beat state. If beat is still in an 'active' state (not queue/terminal), send a followup prompt instructing the agent to advance or roll back. If already advanced, return false (let the grace-period close handler finish the session).
3. The handler uses runtime.sendUserTurn(child, prompt, 'take_loop_follow_up') rather than writing directly to stdin.
4. A test in src/lib/__tests__/terminal-manager-take-child.test.ts (new or existing) verifies: a mock child emits a turn-ended signal → onTurnEnded fires → sendUserTurn is called with a followup prompt when the beat is still active.
5. Test also verifies the handler DOES NOT send a followup when the beat has already advanced.
6. All lint/typecheck/test/build pass.
7. Depends on and lands after knots-a401 — check with kno show a401 before claiming implementation.
